name: masscan
title: masscan
summary: masscan - Fast scan of the Internet
description:
TCP port scanner
MASSCAN is TCP port scanner which transmits SYN packets
asynchronously and produces results similar to nmap,
the most famous port scanner. Internally, it operates
more like scanrand, unicornscan, and ZMap, using
asynchronous transmission.
It's a flexible utility that allows arbitrary address and
port ranges.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use masscan because masscan - Fast scan of the Internet.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use masscan as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `masscan <ip addresses/ranges> -p ports options` as documented upstream for masscan.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair masscan with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `masscan --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: masscan - Fast scan of the Internet
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install masscan. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `masscan <ip addresses/ranges> -p ports options`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: masscan - Fast scan of the Internet.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: masscan - Fast scan of the Internet.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install masscan
Install with: sudo apt install masscan
